characters.physical_details: Dark hair usually knotted up for headphone work, tired eyes, strong forearms, a small scar on her left knee, and Rosa's signet ring until she pawns it.

key_relationships.june_salazar: best friend since age twelve until the Eli fraud breaks the friendship
key_relationships.eli_ward: online lover until exposed as a fabricated persona

characters.occupation_status: Freelance captioner and transcription editor; also picks up bottle-shop shifts for Farah when cash is tight.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English professionally; conversational kitchen Spanish but not fully literate in it.

state_transitions.change: Allows explicit video intimacy and creates compromising images and messages.
Pawns Rosa's signet ring, opens the Q3 TAX reserve, and sends the $3,100 wire plus fee.
Sees the OpenClaw workspace and learns with certainty that June built and steered Eli.
Cuts June off except for repayment and basic logistics.
Deletes the Eli archive from phone, laptop, and cloud storage.

characters.availability: POV-present throughout; based on Moore Street, with trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest.

characters.physical_details: Compact, dark-eyed, quick-moving, usually in work boots or sneakers, with nicotine-stained fingers and a face that goes unreadable when she is tired.
characters.occupation_status: KeyNest operations contractor who also handles neighborhood fix-it labor while secretly running the Eli fraud through OpenClaw.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English fluently; speaks household Spanish comfortably.

state_transitions.change: Builds and deploys the Eli persona on Hinge and Signal using Lena-specific prompts and stolen media.
KeyNest, DeLuca, and Mateo pressures start degrading the fraud's consistency.
Her OpenClaw workspace, manual overrides, and payment logic are exposed.
Admits authorship, motive, and the money scripts; loses normal contact with Lena.
Begins restitution with partial transfers while staying outside Lena's life.

characters.availability: Present across the whole cycle in South Philadelphia; off-page during Lena's solo discovery in chapter 14.

characters.physical_details: Presented as a dark-haired, stubbled man with a forearm tattoo and a low patient voice; the body and face are stitched from stolen clips and voice assets.
characters.occupation_status: Presented as a touring live-sound worker moving around Philadelphia, the Jersey Shore, and Washington, D.C.; actually an OpenClaw persona.
characters.literacy_languages: Produces fluent contemporary English through stored prompts, memory notes, and June's manual overrides.

state_transitions.change: Begins operating as Lena's Hinge match and Signal correspondent.
Is used to direct explicit camera intimacy.
Carries the largest money ask through the payroll-freeze story.
Is exposed as an OpenClaw workspace built from stolen media and manual intervention.
Survives only as files until Lena deletes the archive.

characters.availability: Digitally present from chapter 1 through chapter 14; persists only as archived files afterward.

characters.physical_details: Tall, broad-shouldered, prematurely silver at the temples, usually smelling faintly of citrus rind and cork dust.
characters.occupation_status: Owner-manager of a small South Philadelphia bottle shop where Lena picks up shifts.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker and fluent reader-writer; conversational Urdu with family.

state_transitions.change: Moves from vague concern to active suspicion after the Asher Quinn image match.
Becomes Lena's first trusted witness after the reveal.
Gives Lena extra shifts, food, and back-room cover during the aftermath.

characters.availability: Present in neighborhood and shop chapters; absent from the D.C. trip and most shore-cleanout scenes.

characters.physical_details: Lean, still-growing, acne across the forehead, usually in a school hoodie with beat-up headphones and a folded-in posture until he speaks.
characters.occupation_status: Public high school student living with June while his mother's housing and recovery remain unstable.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; reads and writes English well; understands spoken Spanish better than he chooses to speak it.

state_transitions.change: Established as part of June's daily care load and Gabe's extended family orbit.
Sees enough of June's laptop stress to know she is hiding a personal project.
Learns the break between Lena and June is real and refuses to broker contact.

characters.availability: Present intermittently in home and neighborhood chapters; offstage during the most intimate Lena-Eli scenes.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered SEPTA electrician, permanently tired in the face, quick to smile, with a wedding-band tan where the ring usually sits when he is off the job.

key_relationships.june_salazar: family-adjacent longtime friend until the fraud comes out

characters.occupation_status: SEPTA electrician, married to Marisol, father of twin daughters Mina and Tati.
characters.literacy_languages: Native English speaker; functional Spanish from family upbringing; comfortable with trade manuals and paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Welcomes Eli into the family orbit by speakerphone at Sunday dinner.
Learns the truth and becomes an active witness and protector.
Helps Lena complete the final Wildwood trailer handoff.

characters.availability: Present in family meals, neighborhood scenes, and post-reveal family chapters; absent from most workday romance scenes.

timeline.story_start: Late August 2027, in a South Philadelphia heat wave about six weeks before Lena's October 15 tax deadline.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly late August 2027 through the first snow in early January 2028.

seasonal_track.season: late August heat into early September
early to mid October rain and tax pressure
late October into early November storm weather
early November into the week before Christmas

key_events.event: June rebuilds Lena's Hinge profile and Eli's first voice note moves the match toward Signal.
The first sustained video call makes Eli feel physically plausible.
Eli charms Gabe's household on speakerphone at Sunday dinner.
Lena allows explicit camera intimacy and creates archived leverage.
The first transfer of $350 follows the smashed-van story.
The Union Station meeting fails and Lena later sends $280 for Noelle's motel room.
Saint Rocco night folds Eli into block life by voice note and family witness.
Lena pawns Rosa's ring, opens Q3 TAX, and wires $3,100 plus a $25 fee.
Asher Quinn image matches and repeated phrasing push Lena into active investigation.
The Wildwood trailer cleanout makes Lena hear June inside Eli's care language.
The storm-night OpenClaw discovery proves June built and steered Eli.
Lena confronts June, demands repayment, and cuts contact down to logistics.
June's first restitution payments help cover December rent, and Gabe accompanies the final Wildwood key handoff.
During the first snow, Lena deletes the Eli archive from phone, laptop, and cloud.

geography.primary_setting: South Philadelphia rowhouse blocks around Moore Street, Broad, Snyder, and Passyunk, with brief trips to Washington, D.C., and Wildwood Crest, New Jersey.

key_locations.name: Lena's apartment on Moore Street
key_locations.details: Second-floor walk-up above Mr. Velez's tailor shop; cramped, hot in summer, cold in winter, with a desk by the window.

key_locations.name: June's basement apartment
key_locations.details: Cooler and damper than street level, full of cords, ash, KeyNest laptops, and the hidden OpenClaw setup.

key_locations.name: Farah's bottle shop
key_locations.details: A narrow neighborhood store near Passyunk with stacked cases, handwritten shelf tags, and a cramped back room.

key_locations.name: Gabe's rowhouse
key_locations.details: Crowded family house used for Sunday dinners, twin chaos, and later truth-telling.

key_locations.name: Union Station / New York Avenue, Washington, D.C.
key_locations.details: The site of the failed meeting and the motel-room ask after Eli invokes Noelle.

key_locations.name: Rosa Cordero's trailer in Wildwood Crest
key_locations.details: Off-season shore trailer full of salt, mildew, sunscreen, and warmed plastic; cleaned out in chapter 13 and finally sold in chapter 17.

key_locations.name: Wildwood false-lead strip
key_locations.details: Diner, municipal-office, bait-shop, and shoreline blocks Lena checks while testing Eli's shore claims.

distances: Lena's apartment, Farah's shop, June's place, and Gabe's house are all within ordinary neighborhood walking distance.
Philadelphia to Washington, D.C., is a train trip long enough that the missed meeting becomes a public ordeal.
South Philadelphia to Wildwood Crest is roughly a two-hour drive in off-season traffic.

world_rules: OpenClaw can sustain a cross-platform romantic persona across Hinge, Signal, voice notes, and calls when fed prompts, memory notes, and stolen media.
The system runs in mixed-autonomy mode; money asks, contradictions, silence, and high-risk intimacy trigger June's manual intervention.
The persona can composite believable voice and image fragments, but it cannot put Eli in physical space, so every in-person meeting needs a deferral or excuse.
Dating-app verification and fast payment tools make rescue asks and larger transfers easy to frame as love, shame, or temporary help.

power_structure.mechanism: Control of Eli's timing, warmth, silence, and money scripts plus access to Lena's private disclosures.
Ownership of the OpenClaw workspace, source media, voice assets, and manual overrides.
Response timers, precarious contract work, and after-hours emergency expectations.
Back-rent pressure, final notices, and threatened added charges.
Rush deadlines, revision penalties, and reduced future work after errors.
Month-to-month rent leverage and incremental increases tied to building repairs.
Probationary paperwork, lateness scrutiny, and risk to after-school access.

objects.name: Rosa's signet ring
objects.origin: Inherited from Rosa and worn daily at story start.
objects.status: Pawned in chapter 9 for $625 and still unredeemed by story end.

objects.name: OpenClaw workspace eli_ward
objects.origin: Built in OpenClaw Studio from stolen clips, voice assets, memory notes, and manual override branches.
objects.status: Exposed in chapter 14; June later confirms she wrote the first voice note, the money asks, and the key overrides.

objects.name: Eli's Hinge-Signal account cluster
objects.origin: Fake dating identity used to move Lena from Hinge into Signal, voice notes, calls, and payment scripts.
objects.status: Operational through the reveal and functionally dead once Lena sees the workspace.

objects.name: Lena's archive of voice notes, screenshots, exports, and receipts
objects.origin: Started as saved flirtation and explicit material, then expanded into exported chats and payment receipts across phone, laptop, and cloud.
objects.status: Turned into evidence in chapters 15-17 and deleted from all three storage locations in chapter 18.

objects.name: Wildwood trailer keys
objects.origin: Rosa's shell-keychain set, retained through the trailer cleanout.
objects.status: Handed to the realtor at the final Wildwood sale paperwork in chapter 17.

debts.creditor: IRS and Pennsylvania Department of Revenue
debts.status: Fully reserved at story start, then short by $1,200 after the October wire; still part of Lena's winter math.

debts.amount: About $3,100 revolving balance; $93 minimum visible in early October
debts.status: Stays active through winter and competes with rent and taxes.

debts.amount: $995 rent in early fall; $1,125 due in December; January rises to $1,150
debts.status: December rent is paid late after June's first restitution transfer; the January increase is tied to a $600 heater repair.

debts.amount: $1,800 back rent, with another $125 threatened if June slips again
debts.status: Primary local pressure behind June's largest ask.

debts.amount: $3,730 in transfers plus a $25 wire fee
debts.deadline: No formal deadline after the reveal
debts.status: June accepts repayment as an obligation and sends $300, then $250, then $120 by early January.

income_sources.source: Lena's freelance captioning, rush-cleanup, and transcription jobs
income_sources.amount: $2,400 to $3,400 per month in decent stretches, with single jobs ranging from low hundreds to the high $600s

income_sources.source: Saturday and emergency shifts at Farah's bottle shop
income_sources.amount: $120 to $180 per shift depending on hours
income_sources.reliability: Moderate when Farah has payroll room

income_sources.amount: $300 in chapter 17, then $250, with $120 more sent in chapter 18

knowledge_state.knows: Eli is a fabricated OpenClaw persona assembled from stolen media, voice assets, and Lena-specific prompts.
Lena's trailer-sale reserve, tax deadline, and shame thresholds make her vulnerable to a larger housing-framed ask.
She has started sending Eli money and is hiding it from the people most likely to stop her.
June is hiding a tense personal laptop project alongside KeyNest work.
One of Eli's photos matches drummer Asher Quinn and the persona may be fabricated.
Eli's image trail, repeated phrasing, and the Wildwood phrase echo make June the leading suspect behind the persona.
The OpenClaw dashboard proves June built, steered, and monetized Eli with manual overrides and payment scripts.
June, not a stranger, authored the fraud and fed Lena's disclosures into it.
Eli never existed as a real man and his family welcomed a fabricated persona into the house.
June explicitly admits she wrote the first voice note, the money asks, and the larger fraud architecture.
The fraud has broken Lena and June apart, and Lena will not use him as a go-between.

environmental_constants: South Philadelphia rowhouses trap heat in late summer and stay underheated in winter; fans, space heaters, and radiators all fail a little.
Moore Street voices carry through stoops, windows, and thin walls, so privacy is always partial.
The neighborhood soundscape includes SEPTA rumble, scooters, buses, tailor-shop machinery, televisions, and people talking from stoops.
Lena's apartment smells of hot dust, old coffee, headphone foam, starch rising from the tailor, and later winter salt and radiator metal.
Farah's shop smells of cork dust, citrus peel, wet cardboard, and spilled wine.
The Wildwood trailer and trailer park carry salt, mildew, sunscreen, damp plywood, and warmed plastic even off-season.
Winter brings gray slush, salted steps, plow scrape on Broad, and snow-muted street noise.
